title: Persistent vs Ephemeral Agents
summary: A persistent agent is created, versioned, reused, published, and monitored over time. An ephemeral agent or in-process hosted pattern is created for a session or runtime flow and can be discarded after the task.
analogy: A persistent agent is a standing unit with a call sign. An ephemeral agent is a temporary patrol assembled for one mission.
diagram:
graph TD
Need[Agent need] --> Stable[Stable repeated workflow]
Need --> Temporary[One off workflow]
Stable --> Persistent[Persistent versioned agent]
Temporary --> Ephemeral[Ephemeral runtime agent]
Persistent --> Publish[Publish and monitor]
Ephemeral --> Cleanup[Discard after session]
body:
## First principles

First principles: some agents are products and some agents are temporary workers. Products need versioning, endpoints, identity, monitoring, and governance. Temporary workers need fast startup and cleanup.

## Definition

A persistent agent is created, versioned, reused, published, and monitored over time. An ephemeral agent or in-process hosted pattern is created for a session or runtime flow and can be discarded after the task.

## How it fits together

Use persistent prompt agents for stable business assistants, repeatable tools, and audit trails. Use ephemeral patterns for experiments, dynamic one-off orchestration, or framework-hosted agents where the code owns most behavior.

## Worked example

```python
# pip install azure-ai-projects>=2.0.0 azure-identity
import os
from azure.identity import DefaultAzureCredential
from azure.ai.projects import AIProjectClient
from azure.ai.projects.models import PromptAgentDefinition

project = AIProjectClient(endpoint=os.environ["PROJECT_ENDPOINT"], credential=DefaultAzureCredential())
agent_name = os.environ.get("AGENT_NAME", "stable-support-agent")

agent = project.agents.create_version(
    agent_name=agent_name,
    definition=PromptAgentDefinition(
        model=os.environ["MODEL_DEPLOYMENT_NAME"],
        instructions="Stable support agent. Prefer approved tools and answer with traceable reasoning.",
    ),
)
print("Created reusable version", agent.name, agent.version)
# Keep persistent agents. Delete only scratch agents created for experiments.
```

*Create a reusable agent name that can be promoted later*

drills.a: When the agent is reused, governed, published, monitored, or given stable permissions.
For experiments or short-lived workflows where the runtime code owns orchestration.
A production agent can receive a distinct identity and stronger lifecycle governance.

teachItBack: Agent lifetime is an architecture decision. Persistent agents are governed products; ephemeral agents are disposable workers. Pick the lifetime before assigning permissions, because identity and audit needs follow lifetime.

glossary.definition: Agent definition intended to be reused and governed over time.
Short-lived agent instance or in-process flow created for a narrow task.

sources.title: Microsoft Learn: What is Microsoft Foundry Agent Service
Microsoft Learn: Agent identity concepts in Microsoft Foundry
